_meta.source: ux-skill v2 - page-level section sequences (richness module)
_meta.purpose: Whole-page templates keyed by GOAL. The build flow selects one by goal/keywords, then EXPANDS the entire ordered section_sequence and maps ALL source content into it (every sector -> a pill, every size -> a card, every benefit -> a checklist item). Each card/pill/stat gets a relevant inline SVG icon (Lucide-style, never emoji). The conversion_mechanisms are added even when the source page lacked them. The complement to landing-patterns.json (section-level) -- this manifest is the page-level skeleton that sequences those sections.

section_sequence.purpose: Capture the lead at first paint. Text left (headline + one-line value + phone affordance), inline quote or contact form right. The form is the wow moment and the conversion path -- never buried below.
A thin band of 3-5 real, organic numbers (jobs completed, years operating, response time, coverage radius) directly under the hero -- instant credibility before the scroll.
3-5 cards naming the concrete reasons to choose this service (speed, price transparency, licensing, same-day). Each card carries a relevant inline SVG icon, a tight heading, and one sentence.
Every service category / sector the business covers, rendered as a scannable row of pills (each with an inline SVG glyph). Map EVERY sector from the source into a pill -- do not trim to a tidy few.

Every concrete offering (every skip size, every package, every product line) as its own card with an inline SVG icon, spec line, and an action. Completeness over neatness: one source item -> one card.
2-column zig-zag rows that expand the marquee differentiators with depth -- alternating media/text, each row earning its own visual. Not a 3-equal-card grid.
Where the service operates -- a map, a region list, or a coverage statement. For a local/commercial service this answers the 'do you cover me?' question that gates the lead.
A named, quantified testimonial or a strong pull-quote with attribution (name, role/company). One strong quote beats fifteen vibes-only blurbs.
A focused conversion band repeating the primary action (get a quote / call now) with the phone affordance, after the page has earned the click.
A sitemap footer: services, coverage areas, company, contact (phone + hours), legal. Generosity after the page's restraint -- never a thin one-row strip.

entries.cta_placement: Primary lead-capture in the hero (inline form) and repeated in a dedicated CTA band before the footer; phone number persistent in header and CTA band.
entries.footer: Rich sitemap footer: Services, Coverage areas, Company, Contact (phone + hours), Legal.
entries.notes: Models the spirit of ui-ux-pro-max's 'Trust & Authority + Conversion' page but richer and brand-accurate. The inline hero form + proof bar + phone affordance are mandatory conversion mechanisms for a lead-gen service even if the original site lacked them. Map ALL source content in: every sector -> a pill, every size/package -> an item card, every benefit -> a value card or checklist item. Every card/pill/stat gets a relevant inline SVG icon (Lucide-style, no emoji).

section_sequence.purpose: Asymmetric hero: value headline + one-line lede + primary CTA on one side, a real product mock (screenshot or interactive demo) on the other. The mock is the proof.
A single monochrome customer-logo row near the hero -- social proof before the feature tour. Skip if there are no real logos.
3-5 outcome-led cards (not feature lists), each with an inline SVG icon and a concrete claim with a number where possible.
3-5 marquee feature sections, each a 2-column zig-zag with a deep visual (annotated screenshot, before/after, synthetic UI). Depth over a dense feature grid.
The integrations or ecosystem the product plugs into, as a labelled grid of marks. Maps EVERY integration the source lists.
A stats band of real outcomes (latency, time saved, adoption) -- organic numbers, not round marketing figures.
A named, quantified customer quote or mini case study with attribution and a real metric.
A 'starts at $X' teaser linking out to full pricing -- never a full pricing table on the homepage.
A focused sign-up / start-free band repeating the single primary action.
Sitemap footer by audience: Product, Solutions, Resources, Company, Legal.

entries.cta_placement: Single primary CTA (start free / sign up) in the hero, repeated in the CTA band; secondary 'see the demo' allowed once in the hero.
entries.footer: Sitemap footer by audience: Product, Solutions, Resources, Company, Legal.
entries.notes: One primary path. Map every integration into the integrations grid and every differentiator into its own split feature row. Each value card / integration mark / stat gets an inline SVG icon (no emoji).

section_sequence.purpose: Product imagery gallery on one side, buy box (title, price, variant pickers, add-to-cart) on the other. The buy box is the conversion path, above the fold.
A thin band of trust signals: free shipping threshold, returns window, secure checkout, in-stock status. Each with an inline SVG icon.
Cards covering the concrete reasons to buy (materials, warranty, what's in the box), each with an inline SVG icon and a spec line.
Every variant, size, or option as a selectable card/swatch. Map EVERY option the source lists -- one variant, one tile.
Zig-zag rows telling the product story with real photography or detail shots -- craft, use, results.
Real, attributed reviews with a rating distribution -- named, specific, not 'amazing!'.
A small, curated set of related products -- not an endless grid.
The objection-handling section: shipping, returns, sizing, care. Answers the questions that block checkout.
A sticky or repeated add-to-cart band so the conversion action is always reachable.
Sitemap footer: Shop, Help (shipping/returns), Company, Legal, contact.

entries.cta_placement: Add-to-cart in the buy box above the fold, made persistent via a sticky bar and repeated in a CTA band.
entries.footer: Sitemap footer: Shop, Help (shipping & returns), Company, Legal, contact.
entries.notes: Conversion is the buy box. Vary image aspect ratios; never clip every shot to 1:1. Map every variant into the option grid; every spec into a card. Icons on trust signals and spec cards are inline SVG, no emoji.

section_sequence.purpose: A confident positioning statement with a peek of selected work. Editorial, asymmetric -- the work carries the page.
An irregular, editorial grid of case studies (varied aspect ratios, generous gaps). Each tile is a real project, not a card box.
What the studio does, as capability cards or a list -- each with an inline SVG icon. Map EVERY discipline the source lists.
1-3 deep case studies as zig-zag rows: problem, approach, result with a real metric.
How the studio works -- a few honest steps, not numbered chapter signposting for its own sake.
A monochrome client-logo wall or named testimonials with attribution.
Who's behind the work -- real people, real photos or styled portraits.
A direct 'start a project / let's talk' band with a real contact path.
Sitemap footer: Work, Services, About, Contact, social.

entries.cta_placement: Primary 'start a project / get in touch' in the hero secondary slot and repeated in a dedicated CTA band; contact details in the footer.
entries.footer: Sitemap footer: Work, Services, About, Contact, social.
entries.notes: The work is the proof; description is supplementary. Use varied image ratios and an irregular grid -- never a tidy 3-equal-card row. Every capability and stat gets an inline SVG icon, no emoji.

section_sequence.purpose: A masthead with the lead story given editorial weight -- big headline, real image, byline. Sets the publication's voice.
An editorial grid of featured pieces with varied prominence (one dominant, several secondary) -- not equal cards.
Every section/topic the publication covers, as a row of pills (each with an inline SVG glyph). Map EVERY category from the source.
A scannable list of recent pieces with byline, date, and reading time -- typographic, not boxed.
A highlighted piece or a pull-quote that carries the editorial point of view, with attribution.
An inline newsletter capture (not a timed modal) with a clear value line and a single email field.
Who writes here -- real contributors with names and roles.
A focused subscribe band repeating the newsletter or membership action.
Sitemap footer: Sections, Archive, About, Subscribe, legal.

entries.cta_placement: Inline newsletter / subscribe capture mid-page and repeated in a CTA band; never a first-paint modal.
entries.footer: Sitemap footer: Sections, Archive, About, Subscribe, legal.
entries.notes: Typography and hierarchy carry the page. Map every topic into a category pill and every recent piece into the list. Newsletter capture is inline, never a timed interstitial. Icons are inline SVG, no emoji.

section_sequence.purpose: A device mock (real app screen) beside a value headline and app-store badges. The store badges are the conversion path, above the fold.
A thin band of real numbers (downloads, rating, active users) -- organic, not round marketing figures.
3-5 cards naming what the app does for the user, each with an inline SVG icon and a concrete benefit.
Real app screenshots in device frames showing the key flows -- varied, not all clipped to one ratio.
Zig-zag rows expanding the marquee features with a screenshot each -- depth over a dense grid.
Named, specific app-store reviews or a rating breakdown -- not 'great app!'.
A short, honest set of steps from install to value.
A focused download band repeating the store badges and the single primary action.
Sitemap footer: App, Support, Company, Legal, store links.

entries.cta_placement: App-store badges in the hero and repeated in a download CTA band; rating shown near the badges as a trust signal.
entries.footer: Sitemap footer: App, Support, Company, Legal, store links.
entries.notes: Conversion is the store badge. Use real device-framed screenshots with varied crops. Map every feature into a split row and every screen into the gallery. Icons on value cards and trust signals are inline SVG, no emoji.
